reviews.review_title: Dont play it! Addicting but disapointing
reviews.review_description: First of i would just like to say that this game is amazing. It gives you a great work out, the graphics are quite stunning whilst playing on the meta quest 2, and the controlls and mechanics overal are perfect. However, (here it comes :(. ) the game was released for free as a trial before the launch of the actual full game. It was released in 2023 and now nearing 2026 we still do not have the full game.... that is the only real downside i have with this game. Its still a beta. Nearly 3 years passed since then and no full game release yet.... its one of those games i would gladly lay down 20/25 euros for. If it released. So overall i guess the only peoblem is they have left us longing for more with no date for an actual full release.
The movement is bad, the dash is useless, it ragebaits you soo much and if you fall a little bit then you start back at the beginning. PLEASE DONT DOWNLOAD

This is the first game I completed on my quest3. It is very engaging and challenging. The only problem is that on the triangular shapes it is difficult. But I guess that it increases the challenge level .
waiting for the full version

I barely got through the tutorial, I kept getting pulled off platforms and pulled backwards, the hand movement sensitivity is wayyy too sensitive. I found a way to move without getting dragged back too many times which is windmilling your arms but even that's not guaranteed.

Jumping is weird too, one little push on the ground, like literally just putting both hands down carefully will launch you into space in any direction, it will also throw you In a random direction sometimes even when aiming for a spot.

Overall it's a good idea but the mechanics seem like gorilla tag ordered from wish

I started playing this game not expecting to like it. Not sure how high it goes but now I can't quit till I see. Also, movement is a little difficult but once you get the hang of it, you can move around without exerting very much effort. Compared to how exhausted I was getting at first, figuring that out made it more enjoyable.

I LOOOOVE games that make you move. The fact that the map just keep changing as you go up, and the mood music changes too just makes you want to keep going! 
If I can suggest one thing: Get rid of the artificial jump, just make it physical like the game GRAB. You can be more precise and it feels great.
Best game I ever played. So much fun to play and very creative. 10/10

So good fun thanks for who ever made it

reviews.review_title: It’s a scam, and it’s bad
reviews.review_description: What you see in the title is not in game, you don’t walk because of COURSE ITS A “GORILLA TAG LIKE GAME” Like bro PLEASE, get creative and ACTUALLY make a good game because I don’t have room to swing my arms through my pc, immediately I wanted to walk and dodge things in air and it’s just swing your arms, then I just left the game. Very bad game don’t recommend.
I land and just get flung i use the hands to walk and does nothing or just jumps 0/10

Movement was terrible, and moving objects were really glitchy. I guess I kept getting too close to objects that I never hit and got respwned. Hit boxes on objects was really terrible as well. FIX YOUR GAME CAUSE IT SUCKS!!!
Please add check points this game sucks don’t waste your time

This is the first time i’ve ever made a review on a vr game because of how shockingly absurd the movement is. If you want to make you game actually decent to play just rip the gorilla tag movement because it would make this game 10x better and make it not play like a tumble dryer being hauled off a cliff. And even the SLIGHTEST TAP with both hands causes you to fly like your some rip off iron man it’s actually so irritating

We all know it's a ripoff, but that isn't the issue. I usually love games remade in VR, but the controls are SO BAD.

If you've played gorilla tag, it's similar, just worse. You jump with both hands hitting the ground, not one. To walk, it's the same is gorilla tag, but you're practically stuck to the floor and glide across it when you do so.

There's also a boost mechanic and it's even more broken than the walking. You press the triggers midair to boost, but sometimes it doesn't even try. It will literally propel you downward for no reason and make you lose a lot of progress.

I've gotten up to the subway trains where the music changes, but the frequent jumps + boost makes it super easy to fall considering how broken all the mechanics are. I fell 3 different times from there, all because of the boost not working properly, and I climbed all the way back up each time.

On my last ascension, I tried to pause it when I got back to the trains. That's how I learned the pause button takes you back to the beginning for no reason. Yeah.

Obviously I gave up because I had JUST gotten back to my spot and it reset.

I'm not gonna say this isn't worth a shot, because the idea of the game is cool. Had the mechanics not been broken, this could've been a solid knockoff of only up that I would play regardless of its originality.

I could care less if you completely ripoff a game, but at least TRY to polish it. This is the only game I've ever played on oculus when the jumping mechanic is shotty, and that says a lot.
